element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic exploration and combat with flute-based attacks

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game (player stumbles into first Dark Space)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Save points, health recovery, form switching, ability granting
Story exposition; guides Will's quest to save world from approaching comet
Dark knight form with sword combat, higher damage output than Will, longer reach
Energy manifestation form, highest damage output, pseudopod attacks; initially incomplete ability set in development
Collectible artifacts required to progress; each ruin holds a statue piece for Tower of Babel puzzle
Certain areas inaccessible without specific character form; encourages revisiting with new abilities
Themed dungeons (Incan ruins, Angkor Wat, Great Wall of China, Egyptian pyramids, Tower of Babel)
Non-RPG progression; permanent stat increases (attack, defense, health) via jewel collection

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early awareness, but completion dependent on early exploration
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional collectible; missable if not found before advancing story
Scarce consumable healing resources; creates resource management tension
Previous comet approaches disrupted world evolution; current approach tied to Blazer War

element_introduction_order.element: Will as Dark Knight / Kara as Light Knight
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ultimate story revelation; enables Shadow form union and firebird summon

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating all enemies in a room (jewel reward)
Successful form transformation for puzzle solution
typical_tension_triggers: Entering new ruin with unknown layout
Low health with scarce herb availability
Character-gated areas (inability to progress without transformation)
Room-based combat encounters with multiple enemies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven pacing with punctuated combat encounters; tension builds through resource scarcity (herbs) and area gating; relief provided by Dark Space checkpoints and story beats

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Red Jewels scattered throughout accessible areas; off-path exploration rewards
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Character form abilities unlock previously inaccessible areas; encourages systematic revisiting of earlier ruins

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like introduction to Will's basic combat and exploration; Dark Space mechanic taught early; low enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of transformations (Freedan) expands combat options; character-gated puzzles increase spatial complexity; Mystic Statue collection quest spans this period
difficulty_curve.late_game: Shadow joins party with highest damage output; Tower of Babel climax with story revelation; final boss (Dark Gaia manifested comet); fixed difficulty setting maintained throughout
notable_spikes: Freedan introduction mid-game increases combat demand
Tower of Babel final sequence combines all learned mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Real-world location reimagined as fantasy dungeon (Incan ruins, Angkor Wat, Great Wall, Egyptian pyramids). Each contains puzzle-solving with character abilities and enemy combat encounters. Each ruin holds a Mystic Statue piece.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Angkor Wat, Great Pyramid, Great Wall of China

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe area within dungeons and exploration zones. Functions as save point, health recovery station, form-switching area, and ability-granting location. Encounters with Gaia and statue manifestations.
Linear progression through rooms with enemy encounters. Defeating all enemies in a room grants a jewel reward (stat increase). Enemies reappear on area re-entry but no bonus re-awarded.
Certain areas inaccessible without specific character form (Will, Freedan, Shadow). Teaches player to recognize form requirements and encourages planned revisiting.
Open-world-like exploration of fantasy Earth during Age of Exploration era. Story progression unlocks access to new regions and NPCs. Cannot revisit previous areas except in final third of game.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Overworld between major ruins; hometown, European locations
